examples.title: Human-in-the-Loop Decision System
examples.description: Demonstrates a basic HITL framework where AI proposes decisions and humans approve or override
examples.code:
ALGORITHM HITL_Decision
INPUT: user_request, ai_model, human_reviewer
BEGIN
  ai_proposal ← ai_model.generate_proposal(user_request)
  IF ai_proposal.confidence > HIGH_THRESHOLD THEN
    RETURN ai_proposal
  ELSE
    display(ai_proposal) to human_reviewer
    human_decision ← human_reviewer.evaluate(ai_proposal)
    IF human_decision.approved THEN
      RETURN ai_proposal
    ELSE
      RETURN human_decision.modified_output
    ENDIF
  ENDIF
END

ALGORITHM Human_Override
INPUT: decision, override_request, authority_level
BEGIN
  IF authority_level >= REQUIRED_LEVEL THEN
    log_override(decision, override_request)
    RETURN override_request.new_output
  ELSE
    RETURN decision  // Override rejected
  ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows a HITL system where high-confidence AI decisions can proceed automatically, while lower-confidence decisions require human review. It also demonstrates an override mechanism with authority levels for accountability.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per decision (excluding AI model complexity)

examples.title: Simple Bias Detection System
examples.description: Demonstrates a basic bias detection algorithm that analyzes AI model outputs for demographic parity
examples.explanation: This Python code implements a basic bias detection system that calculates demographic parity across protected attributes. It identifies potential bias when the difference in positive outcomes exceeds a threshold.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n * m) where n is predictions and m is protected attributes

examples.title: Trust Scoring Algorithm
examples.description: Shows how to calculate and maintain trust scores for AI system recommendations
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_Trust_Score
INPUT: ai_output, user_feedback_history, system_performance_metrics
BEGIN
  // Component 1: Historical accuracy of similar predictions
  accuracy_score ← calculate_historical_accuracy(ai_output.category)
  
  // Component 2: Confidence of the model
  confidence_score ← ai_output.confidence_level
  
  // Component 3: User feedback on similar outputs
  feedback_score ← calculate_feedback_score(user_feedback_history, ai_output.category)
  
  // Component 4: Recent system performance
  performance_score ← system_performance_metrics.recent_accuracy
  
  // Weighted combination
  trust_score ← (accuracy_score * 0.3) + (confidence_score * 0.3) +
                (feedback_score * 0.2) + (performance_score * 0.2)
  
  RETURN {
    overall_score: trust_score,
    recommendation: IF trust_score > HIGH_THRESHOLD THEN "USE"
                    ELSE IF trust_score > MEDIUM_THRESHOLD THEN "REVIEW"
                    ELSE "MANUAL"
  }
END

ALGORITHM Update_Trust_After_Decision
INPUT: ai_output, actual_outcome
BEGIN
  error ← abs(ai_output.predicted_value - actual_outcome)
  trust_adjustment ← IF error < TOLERANCE THEN +0.05 ELSE -0.15
  ai_output.trust_score ← clamp(ai_output.trust_score + trust_adjustment, 0, 1)
  RETURN ai_output.trust_score
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates trust scoring by combining multiple factors: historical accuracy, model confidence, user feedback, and system performance. It provides recommendations based on trust levels and updates scores based on actual outcomes.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for maintaining history of trust scores
